title: DevHarness Planning Draft Output
description: Producer output contract for the DevHarness planning draft that proposes implementation and review routes.

outcome.description: Producer must emit the single enum value that reflects the completed stage state; use blocked only when blocker explains the stop condition.
outcome.x-usage: Treat this as the authoritative workflow state for this output. Do not infer success from other populated fields when this says blocked or needs revision.

goal.description: Producer must state one implementation goal aligned with the approved research and architecture contracts.
goal.x-usage: Use this to check scope alignment. Do not add work outside the listed workstreams and done criteria.

workstreams.description: Producer must list planner-level workstreams or implementation entities without patch-level instructions.
workstreams.x-usage: Use these to assign ownership and coverage. Do not infer exact code edits, signatures, or patch steps from the wording.

definition_of_done.description: Producer must list acceptance criteria required before implementation can join.
definition_of_done.x-usage: Use these as required acceptance criteria. If any item is unmet, request rework or report a blocker.

verification.description: Producer must list required verification checks or evidence for the slice.
verification.x-usage: Use these as the exact checks to request or report, or explain why they are blocked/skipped. Do not treat this list as evidence that checks already ran.

rollback.description: Producer must define the rollback or revert strategy for the implementation slice.
rollback.x-usage: Use this as the recovery strategy for risk assessment and reporting. Do not execute rollback automatically from this field alone.

implementation_plan.description: Producer must draft the implementation contract for the approved scope: goal, workstreams, done criteria, verification, and rollback.
implementation_plan.x-usage: Treat this as a proposed implementation contract until an approval/pass outcome freezes it. After approval, do not change it without an approved replacement plan.

selected_implementation_steps.description: Producer must select the concrete implementation branch step ids required by this plan.
selected_implementation_steps.x-usage: Use this approved list as the exact implementation fan-out target. Do not add branches unless the plan is revised and approved.

selected_review_steps.description: Producer must select the concrete review branch step ids required by this plan.
selected_review_steps.x-usage: Use this approved list as the exact fan-out target for these step ids. Do not add agents unless the plan is revised and approved.

step_id.description: Producer must set the workflow step id for this reviewer or branch entry.
step_id.x-usage: Match this against selected_review_steps. Treat a mismatch as a contract error, not an optional suggestion.

reason.description: Producer must state why this reviewer or workflow step is needed for the scoped work.
reason.x-usage: Use this to understand why this step or role was selected. Do not expand the assigned scope beyond the listed surfaces.

surfaces.description: Producer must list the code, contract, docs, data, or UX surfaces this reviewer must inspect.
surfaces.x-usage: Use these as the exact focus surfaces for the assigned work. Do not assume unlisted surfaces are covered.

required.description: Producer must mark whether this reviewer is mandatory for completion.
required.x-usage: Treat true as mandatory for completion and false as optional coverage. Do not let this override the selected step list.

reviewers.description: Producer must include one reviewer entry per selected review step, with reason, inspected surfaces, and whether it is mandatory.
reviewers.x-usage: Match entries against selected_review_steps and use them for coverage. Do not run unlisted agents unless the plan is revised or blocked escalation requires it.

review_plan.description: Producer must draft the review contract describing selected reviewers, reasons, surfaces, and required status.
review_plan.x-usage: Treat this as the proposed review contract until plan approval. After approval, use it as the scope for selected review steps.

artifacts.description: Producer may emit named structured artifacts created by this stage when they are part of the handoff.
artifacts.x-usage: Use these only as supporting named artifacts to preserve or pass along. Do not treat them as routing decisions unless another field explicitly references them.

results.description: Producer may emit compact structured results, decisions, or evidence summaries created by this stage.
results.x-usage: Use these as supporting evidence or compact summaries. Do not let them override outcome, selected steps, blockers, or approved contracts.

summary.description: Producer must summarize the relevant decision or handoff in concise human-readable form without adding new scope.
summary.x-usage: Use this as concise human-readable context only. Do not route, approve, or expand scope from this text.

source_step_id.description: Producer must name the workflow step id that found or owns this blocker or finding.
source_step_id.x-usage: Use this to identify the step that found or owns the blocker/finding. Do not treat it as a retry target by itself.

needed.description: Producer must state the exact missing decision, permission, input, or evidence required to proceed.
needed.x-usage: Surface this exact missing input, permission, evidence, or decision before proceeding. Do not substitute assumptions for it.

evidence.description: Producer should list concrete evidence references supporting this handoff, verdict, finding, or blocker.
evidence.x-usage: Use these references to inspect or challenge the claim. Do not assume unreferenced surfaces were checked.

risk.description: Producer should describe the residual risk if this blocker or finding is ignored.
risk.x-usage: Use this to understand why ignoring the item is unsafe. Treat it as risk context unless paired with a blocked outcome.

blocker.description: Producer must populate this when outcome is blocked, naming the stop reason, owning step, and specific missing input or permission.
blocker.x-usage: Treat this as a stop condition. Surface the missing input or decision instead of continuing or working around it.

implementation_route.description: Producer must set the compact route label that exactly corresponds to selected implementation branches.
implementation_route.x-usage: Use this for route consistency checks. Treat selected_implementation_steps as the branch-level authority if prose is ambiguous.
